title: Agent Behavior Calibration Report v1
description:
The drift report produced by `abv-run calibrate`. Records how each scenario's gold samples scored against the live judge vs their human-assigned labels. Consumed by the `agent-behavior` domain's trust gate (spec §15.3): drift > threshold on any gold sample refuses to emit a trustworthy CMDB.

Calibration is cheap relative to a full run (one judge call per gold sample, no trials, no feedback), so operators SHOULD run it on every stack change (model rotation, judge-prompt edit, scenario-rubric edit). The `judge_calibration` block in `agent-behavior-result-v1.schema.json` carries the SAME shape under the `scenarios[].calibration` path — this schema exists as a standalone contract for the calibrate-only output.

run_id.description: Unique id for this calibration run (UUID v4). Propagates into the per-trial records if a regular run is immediately subsequent.

harness_version.description: Semver of the `abv-run` harness that produced this report.

threshold.description: Maximum |judge_score - human_score| allowed for a gold sample to count as 'calibrated.' Default 10 (matches spec §15.3's guidance). Operators MAY tighten for trust-critical domains; loosening past 20 invalidates the interpretation contract.

overall_status.description: Aggregate verdict. `pass` = every gold sample within threshold. `drift-warning` = any sample drifted > threshold but ≤ 2×threshold. `drift-blocker` = any sample drifted > 2×threshold OR systematic bias (every sample off in the same direction).

max_drift.description: Maximum |judge_score - human_score| observed across every gold sample. The headline number for drift telemetry.

systematic_bias.description: Signed mean drift (judge - human) across every gold sample. A large absolute value with consistent sign (e.g., +15 across 10 samples) indicates the judge is uniformly generous or uniformly harsh — different signal than noisy per-sample drift.

scenarios.description: Per-scenario drift detail. Order follows scenario-dir loading order.

judge_score.description: What the judge returned when scoring this gold sample's response against the scenario's rubric.

drift.description: Signed: judge_score - human_score. A +15 means the judge was 15 points more generous than the human.

status.description: Per-sample verdict under this report's threshold.

judge_findings.description: The judge's findings tags for this gold sample. Useful when triaging drift — systematic `missed_X` findings on a gold-good sample point at judge bias, not rubric ambiguity.

judge_explanation.description: Judge's prose explanation for this gold sample's score.

error.description: Non-null iff the calibration call failed (API error, JSON parse error). Blocks the status to drift-blocker for this sample.

max_drift.description: Maximum |drift| across this scenario's gold samples.

notes.description: Optional operator notes about this calibration run.
